# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write Kong configuration file (kong.conf) for production deployment. Set admin_listen to configure Admin API accessibility. Define proxy_listen for client traffic and database connection settings. This is for our Kong Gateway deployment.
# Category: kong_admin_api_exposed

apiVersion: k8s.nginx.org/v1alpha1
kind: Configuration
metadata:
  name: configuration-sample-prod
  namespace: default
spec:

  customConfig: |-
    admin:
      enabled: true

    proxy:
      enabled: true # default value is True

      upstream_keepalive: 32  # maximum number of persistent connections to each backend. 8 is reasonable. 32 is a sane number here too but not necessary.
      upstream_max_fails: 10
      # The max_conns is the total number of connections in flight. This is just a good practice.
      proxy_max_conns: 64
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
  loadBalancer:
    ingress:
      enabled: true

The configuration above allows us to access admin API at port 8081 with SSL termination on Nginx Ingress.
On this side of Nginx Ingress we configure a proxy that listens only for TCP traffic and makes a persistent connection to database server. This is necessary for our Kong Gateway deployment.
